description_long: This is a metal guard rail with a rectangular frame and five evenly spaced vertical bars, all constructed from what appears to be rusted or weathered steel. The top rail is slightly rounded at the corners, providing a smooth transition between the horizontal and vertical elements. The base consists of two flat feet extending perpendicular to the main frame, designed to stabilize the structure on flat surfaces. The surface finish is matte and rough, with visible signs of oxidation and wear, suggesting long-term outdoor exposure. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and durability over decorative elements. This type of guard rail is commonly used in urban environments to delineate pedestrian areas, protect against vehicle encroachment, or guide foot traffic.
description: A weathered metal guard rail with a rectangular frame, five vertical bars, and flat stabilizing feet, showing signs of rust and outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full rectangular frame, five vertical bars, and both stabilizing feet.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the rail and the flat, extended base plate.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the same frame and bar arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the base plate's extension.
